title: New Screen Time Guidelines for Young Children
subtitle: 两岁前禁屏幕？美国儿科学会新指南说了什么

paragraphs.en: If there's one rule that most parents cling to in the confusing, fast-changing world of kids and media, it's "No screens before age 2." As of today, that rule has been thrown out the window. The American Academy of Pediatrics (AAP), which first issued that recommendation back in 1999, has extensively updated and revised its guidelines for children and adolescents to reflect new research and new habits.
paragraphs.zh: 在儿童与媒体这个令人困惑又日新月异的领域，如果说大多数父母都坚守着一条规则，那就是“两岁前不看屏幕”。时至今日，这条规则已被彻底抛弃。早在1999年首次提出该建议的美国儿科学会，现已对其儿童与青少年指南进行了广泛更新与修订，以反映新的研究成果和新的生活习惯。

paragraphs.en: The new guidelines, especially for very young children, shift the focus from WHAT is on the screen to WHO else is in the room. And in doing so, they raise some intriguing points about the future of learning from media. For babies younger than 18 months, AAP still says no screens at all are the best idea — with one notable exception: Live video chat.
paragraphs.zh: 新指南，尤其是针对低龄儿童的部分，将关注点从屏幕上“有什么”转向了房间里“还有谁”。这一转变也引发了关于未来媒体学习方式的一些有趣思考。对于18个月以下的婴儿，美国儿科学会仍认为完全不看屏幕是最佳选择——但有一个显著例外：实时视频聊天。

words.meaning: adj. 有趣的；引人入胜的

paragraphs.en: Surveys indicate that families already popularly believe that "Face time doesn't count", or at least that the benefit of virtual visits with grandparents or other relatives outweighs the potential cost of exposing babies to the laptop or smartphone. The AAP doesn't cite positive evidence that infants actually get something out of this kind of "conversation", the way that they clearly do from live social interaction.
paragraphs.zh: 调查显示，许多家庭已经普遍认为“视频通话不算看屏幕”，或者至少认为与祖父母或其他亲戚进行虚拟探视的益处，超过了让婴儿接触笔记本电脑或智能手机的潜在代价。美国儿科学会并未引用确凿证据证明婴儿真能从这种“对话”中获益，毕竟他们从实时社交互动中获得的益处是显而易见的。

paragraphs.en: But there's some observational research that infants as young as six months old are emotionally engaged by playing live peekaboo with Grandma online. For infants and toddlers, ages 15 months to 2 years old, there's limited evidence from a couple of very small studies that they can learn new words from educational media, if and only if parents are watching alongside them, repeating what the video says and/or drawing attention to what is on the screen.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，一些观察性研究表明，六个月大的婴儿在与奶奶进行在线躲猫猫游戏时，情感上确实会有所投入。对于15个月至2岁的婴幼儿，几项极小规模的研究提供了有限证据，表明他们能够从教育媒体中学到新词汇，但前提是父母必须在旁边陪同观看，重复视频内容并引导孩子关注屏幕上的画面。

paragraphs.en: In other words, treating a video or an app like a picture book is the best bet. The flip side of this is that many studies have actually shown poorer language skills correlated with earlier solo viewing of "educational" videos. There's also research that shows language delays in children who watch more TV and start watching earlier.
paragraphs.zh: 换句话说，将视频或应用程序当作绘本来看待是最佳策略。然而另一方面，许多研究实际上表明，过早独自观看“教育”视频与较差的语言能力存在关联。还有研究显示，看电视更多且开始看电视年龄更早的儿童，更容易出现语言发育迟缓。

paragraphs.en: In both cases, the problem seems to be media replacing interaction with people. For this reason, the new AAP guideline has changed from "avoid all screens under age 2" to "avoid solo media use in this age group." For preschoolers ages 2 to 5, there's more evidence that they have the ability to transfer knowledge from screens to the real world, including early literacy and math.
paragraphs.zh: 这两种情况中，问题的根源似乎都是媒体取代了人与人之间的互动。正因如此，美国儿科学会的新指南已从“避免两岁以下接触任何屏幕”改为“避免该年龄段儿童独自使用媒体”。对于2至5岁的学龄前儿童，则有更多证据表明他们有能力将屏幕上的知识迁移到现实世界中，包括早期识字和数学能力。

paragraphs.en: For this age group, AAP recommends no more than an hour a day of screen use. And, just as with younger children, they want caregivers to take part in screen time: "Co-view with your children, help children understand what they are seeing, and help them apply what they learn to the world around them."
paragraphs.zh: 针对这一年龄段，美国儿科学会建议每天屏幕使用时间不超过一小时。同时，与更小的孩子一样，他们希望看护者能够参与到屏幕时间中来：“与孩子共同观看，帮助他们理解所见内容，并引导他们将所学知识应用到周围的世界中去。”
